subject: re: Mr. Epstein - LSJ Library & Pool Woodworking
body:
I am very concerned about what has come to light over the past six
weeks.  I have been asking your office for an accurate  accounting of
costs (including evidence of wire transfers sent to Fancelli) and
pictures regarding Fancelli 's work related to LSJ Library and Pool
Woodworking since February 1st.    Your office took two weeks to supply
an accounting of Fancelli's  costs to date.  This information was both
vague and unverifiable.  I continued to ask for receipts and backup to
help me understand how Fancelli spent $800,000 and $88,000 Euro on the
library and pool.  When I asked for pictures to present to Mr. Epstein
you specifically told me that you needed two weeks to obtain pictures
since if anyone other than you took them they would not be clear to Mr.
Epstein.  It is now March 10, 2008 and I have not received pictures nor
have I been provided backup of how Mr. Epstein's $880,000 Euro has been
spent.  Both your response and Doug Stine's response to me was that no
other client has ever been provided this much detail and it would take
Fancelli weeks to provide backup.  Unfortunately, that does not give me
much confidence to the accuracy of Fancelli's numbers you presented to
me.

 

Around February 15th I was called into Mr. Epstein's office as you were
having a meeting with him.  We were discussing Fancelli and  I
specifically asked you if you sent the full amount of proceeds to
Fancelli for both the library and pool woodworking  when it was received
by our office on December 20th 2006.  Your response was that you did
send Fancelli the full amount ($1,050,000 for library and $379,500 for
pool) immediately after it was received by your Paris office.  It is
regrettable that this was not TRUE.  After numerous requests to your
office last week I finally received evidence of your wire transfers made
to Fancelli and it became apparent why it took six weeks to receive this
information. 

 

It appears you have given me no other alternative than to move forward
with outside counsel.  I will ask one last time for the following
information:  

a)      Pictures of library and pool woodworking

b)      Verifiable backup to support Fancelli spending for Library
($800,000 Euro ) and Pool ($88,000)

 

 

Rich Kahn

New York Strategy Group LLC

457 Madison Avenue, 4th Floor

New York, NY 10021

tel 212-891-6430

fax 212-750-2408

cell 917-414-7584

I am very concerned about what has come to light over the past six weeks.  I have been asking your office for an accurate  accounting of costs (including evidence of wire transfers sent to Fancelli) and pictures regarding Fancelli ‘s work related to LSJ Library and Pool Woodworking since February 1st.    Your office took two weeks to supply an accounting of Fancelli’s  costs to date.  This information was both vague and unverifiable.  I continued to ask for receipts and backup to help me understand how Fancelli spent $800,000 and $88,000 Euro on the library and pool.  When I asked for pictures to present to Mr. Epstein you specifically told me that you needed two weeks to obtain pictures since if anyone other than you took them they would not be clear to Mr. Epstein.  It is now March 10, 2008 and I have not received pictures nor have I been provided backup of how Mr. Epstein’s $880,000 Euro has been spent.  Both your response and Doug Stine’s response to me was that no other client has ever been provided this much detail and it would take Fancelli weeks to provide backup.  Unfortunately, that does not give me much confidence to the accuracy of Fancelli’s numbers you presented to me.

 

Around February 15th I was called into Mr. Epstein’s office as you were having a meeting with him.  We were discussing Fancelli and  I specifically asked you if you sent the full amount of proceeds to Fancelli for both the library and pool woodworking  when it was received by our office on December 20th 2006.  Your response was that you did send Fancelli the full amount ($1,050,000 for library and $379,500 for pool) immediately after it was received by your Paris office.  It is regrettable that this was not TRUE.  After numerous requests to your office last week I finally received evidence of your wire transfers made to Fancelli and it became apparent why it took six weeks to receive this information.

 

It appears you have given me no other alternative than to move forward with outside counsel.  I will ask one last time for the following information: 

a)      Pictures of library and pool woodworking

b)      Verifiable backup to support Fancelli spending for Library ($800,000 Euro ) and Pool ($88,000)

 

 

Rich Kahn
